title: Bulbocodium subluteum
taxonomy: Bulbocodium subluteum is classified within the family Colchicaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Bulbocodium, which consists of several species of autumn-flowering plants. The Colchicaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as autumn-crocus or meadow saffron. The classification of Bulbocodium subluteum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Within the plant kingdom, the species Bulbocodium subluteum is a flowering perennial native to the Iberian Peninsula.

intro: Bulbocodium subluteum, also known as the Yellow Winter Aconite, is a poisonous perennial plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbocodium subluteum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Bulbocodium subluteum is often used to make preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat conditions like eczema, acne, and wounds, although it is essential to note that its use can be hazardous if not properly handled due to its toxicity.
